title: xeraea brownii
intro: Xeraea brownii, also known as Brown's Xeraea or Brown's Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Xeraea brownii include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Xeraea brownii can be made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the oil from the plant to use topically or internally.
